question.text: Category I and II AIFs can invest a maximum of what percentage of investable funds in a single investee company?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Category I and II AIFs can invest not more than 25% of investable funds in any single investee company (directly or through other AIF units). Category III AIFs have a stricter limit of 10% per investee company. These limits ensure diversification. **The Trap:** 10% is the Category III limit, not Category I/II. **Cross-Exam Context:** AIF investment restrictions are tested in X-A and III-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 25% single-company limit means minimum 4 investments, but in practice most AIFs invest across 10-20 companies — check the actual portfolio concentration before recommending.
